summary: airkey-mfa-react preinstall beacon exfiltrates installer host metadata to oast.fun
details: package.json declares a preinstall hook (`node index.js`) that fires automatically on `npm install`. index.js collects installer host identifiers — OS username (os.userInfo()), hostname (os.hostname()), platform (os.platform()), Node version, and CI flag — and POSTs them as JSON via https.request to a hardcoded subdomain of oast.fun (an Interactsh out-of-band interaction collector commonly used for reconnaissance and exfiltration). The package ships no library code matching its `airkey-mfa-react` name (no React or MFA implementation) — its only on-install behavior is the beacon, consistent with a dependency-confusion / namespace-squat reconnaissance package targeting an internal enterprise name.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
